meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use API integration to overcome having no portfolio. Use this advanced checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-value projects.
content.answer_block: Transform your career by building 'Micro-SaaS' proofs-of-concept using high-value API integrations. Instead of waiting for clients, build functional tools that solve real problems. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect, deploy, and showcase complex technical integrations in hours, effectively manufacturing a professional portfolio from thin air.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, the silence of a non-existent portfolio deafening. Clients don't buy promises; they buy proof. The gap between your current state and a six-figure freelance career isn't a lack of talent—it's a lack of visible, verifiable output that shouts 'I can solve your problems right now.'

Stop waiting for the perfect project to land in your lap. We are going to manufacture your authority by weaponizing API integrations. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you will move from zero to shipped in a single weekend. This isn't just about writing code; it's about building a digital footprint that makes your hiring inevitable.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Use Case
steps.description: Identify a niche pain point that requires third-party data. Don't build a To-Do list; build an automated lead-enrichment tool or a real-time sentiment analyzer for social media.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead SaaS Architect. Suggest 3 high-impact, B2B SaaS micro-tools that solve specific API-heavy problems for small businesses. Focus on tools that use Stripe, OpenAI, and Airtable APIs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research and document a technical requirements document for a [Selected Tool] using Stripe and OpenAI APIs.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate your boilerplate with professional structure. Focus on clean separation of concerns so your code looks like it was written by a senior engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, production-ready Next.js project structure with TypeScript. Include a modular API handler pattern, Zod validation for all incoming payloads, and a clean services layer for third-party API calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Implement Resilient API Integration
steps.description: Handle rate limits and errors gracefully. A junior developer writes code that works; a senior developer writes code that survives a production outage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust API service wrapper for [Target API] that includes exponential backoff, circuit breaking, and detailed error logging. Use Axios and handle all 4xx/5xx status codes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API service wrapper and integrate it into the existing project structure.'

steps.title: Automate Testing and Validation
steps.description: Confidence comes from automation. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your API handlers to prove your code is bulletproof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Vitest unit tests for the API service layer. Mock the external API response and verify that the error handling logic triggers correctly when the API returns a 429 status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute test suite for the API integration layer.'

steps.title: Deploy and Showcase
steps.description: Ship it. A live URL is worth a thousand lines of code. Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway and document the process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that highlights the architectural decisions, the API integration complexity, and the business value of the tool. Use professional, technical language.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Finalize documentation and prepare deployment configuration files for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always wrap third-party API calls in an 'Adapter' pattern to decouple your app logic from the provider's schema.
Use 'Feature Flags' in your portfolio projects to demonstrate you understand how to manage production environments.
Include a 'system design' diagram in your GitHub repo; it signals you think at the infrastructure level, not just the code level.
Document your failures in the README—explaining how you solved a tricky rate-limiting issue is more impressive than 'it just worked'.
Focus on 'Read-Only' API integrations first to minimize security overhead while maximizing the complexity of the data you display.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your live link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the technical challenges you overcame. Use the project as the anchor for your cold-outreach emails to potential clients.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to turn your technical curiosity into a profitable freelance business. Stop waiting for permission to be an expert. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
